description: m-2a mockup UI shell: build the fixture-backed library and review shell first, then carry forward the remaining feature testing backlog.

userStories.title: Build UI shell fixture foundation and default app host
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the app to open into a fixture-backed shell so that the mockup-first UI can land without backend or live review wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: Default frontend app renders the new shell host instead of the live review app
Shell data loads through static frontend fixtures and one UI-only loader
Page selection uses local shell state only with no router dependency
No backend contract or `video-review/api.ts` work is added
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Reset Ralph backlog for mockup UI shell, m-2a: Mockup UI Shell, Build UI shell fixture foundation, Review Workspace Ergonomics, Frontend Interaction Spec. Start at frontend/src/app/App.tsx and docs/ui/video-library.html plus docs/ui/video-annotation.html. Keep this UI-only.

userStories.title: Build video library mockup shell
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a mockup-first video library so that I can choose one fixture video and enter the review shell.
acceptanceCriteria: Library shell matches docs/ui/video-library.html with fixture data only
Top header chrome, left nav rail, and summary metrics strip render in the library shell
Each card shows preview, display name, state badge, frame count, fps, resolution, last reviewed label, and one detail line
Propagation progress shows only for fixture rows with state `in_progress`
`Open Review` is present and wired only to local shell navigation
Library shell screenshot matches the mockup `docs/ui/video-library-mockup.png` with no live data or review wiring
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: m-2a: Mockup UI Shell, Build video library mockup shell, Review Workspace Ergonomics. Use docs/ui/video-library.html as visual source. No backend calls.

userStories.title: Build review page mockup shell
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the review page to match the annotation mockup so that the UI shell looks like the agreed product direction before live wiring starts.
acceptanceCriteria: Review shell matches docs/ui/video-annotation.html and docs/ui/video-annotation-mockup.png with fixture data only
Top metadata bar and left global nav render in the review shell
Frame-object list, stage, bottom transport, timeline, and right inspector all render from shell data
Stage chrome includes the visible play overlay and review action controls from the mockup even when they are UI-only
Selected object state drives inspector display locally
Buttons stay UI-only unless they change shell page state
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: m-2a: Mockup UI Shell, Build review page mockup shell, Review Workspace Ergonomics. Use docs/ui/video-annotation.html as visual source. Do not wire live review behavior.

userStories.title: Wire page actions and local UI state
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the shell pages to open and return cleanly so that the library and review mockups behave like one coherent UI shell.
acceptanceCriteria: `Open Review` opens the chosen fixture video in the review shell
Review shell exposes one clear `Back to Library` affordance in its chrome
`Back to Library` returns to the library shell
Selected video and selected object stay coherent across shell page switches
Navigation uses local state only and adds no router library
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: m-2a: Mockup UI Shell, Wire page actions and local UI state, Review Workspace Ergonomics. Keep state local to the shell host. No URL routing.

userStories.title: Add UI integration tests for shell
userStories.description: As a developer, I want durable shell tests so that the mockup-first UI has proof without pulling browser E2E or backend work into the default path.
acceptanceCriteria: Task work re-thinks frontend integration vs browser E2E with the testing notes before choosing coverage
Frontend integration proves library shell chrome render, review shell chrome render, open-review navigation, back-to-library navigation, and selected-object inspector switching
Frontend integration proves the explicit review-screen back affordance exists and works
Tests use mocked HTTP or fixture loads only and do not depend on live backend routes
Browser E2E is absent by default or justified explicitly if one shell workflow truly needs browser proof
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Add UI integration tests for shell, frontend-integration-tests, e2e-tests, Review Workspace Ergonomics. Default proof is frontend integration with fake backend boundary.

userStories.title: Test review workspace ergonomics

Frontend integration covers library render, review render, open-review navigation, back-to-library navigation, and selected-object inspector switching

userStories.notes: Read memories first: Testing review workspace ergonomics, Review Workspace Ergonomics, frontend-integration-tests, backend-api-integration-tests, e2e-tests. This task is about the shell truth first, not the old baseline workspace story.

userStories.title: Review m-2 and m-2a code and grow backlog
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want one milestone-audit loop story so that code is reviewed against m-2 and m-2a, a detailed gap report is written, concrete fix tasks are added, and a new review story is appended after those fixes for the next loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Review current code against `m-2: Review Workspace Completion` and `m-2a: Mockup UI Shell`
Write one detailed durable report note that says what is good, what is missing, what is wrong, and what should improve
Create one todo task note per concrete fix chosen from that report
Append matching fix stories to `tools/ralph/prd.json`
Append one cloned re-review story after the newly added fix stories
Record exactly which report note and which new tasks or stories were created
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Review m-2 and m-2a code and grow backlog, m-2: Review Workspace Completion, m-2a: Mockup UI Shell, Review Workspace Ergonomics, Frontend Interaction Spec, Repo Current State and Feature Matrix. This story does not implement fixes. It audits, writes report, creates fix tasks, appends those stories, then appends a cloned review story after them.

userStories.title: Ship review summary contracts
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want live library and inspector summary payloads so that runtime review screens stop depending on fixture-only status and counters.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes derived library review summary fields from persisted facts instead of fixture-only copy
Backend exposes selected-object summary fields needed for live inspector counters and confidence, or the task records the smallest honest contract split
State derivation stays honest about unshipped export behavior instead of inventing runtime truth
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Ship review summary contracts, Auditing m-2 and m-2a code gaps 2026-04-21, Review Workspace Ergonomics, Video Ingest and Exact-Frame Review, Annotation Foundation and Manual Box Workflow, API, Data Model.

userStories.title: Wire live library shell
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the default library shell to load real backend review summaries so that normal app startup reflects actual indexed work instead of fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Default app library host loads backend review summaries instead of `uiShellFixtureData`
Library cards stay aligned with shell presentation boundaries while using honest live data
`Open Review` selects a real backend video id with no router dependency
Browser verification proves the live library shell works
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Wire live library shell, Auditing m-2 and m-2a code gaps 2026-04-21, Review Workspace Ergonomics, Frontend Interaction Spec, frontend-integration-tests, e2e-tests. Preserve `App.tsx` shell-first host and `shell-host.tsx` page-state boundaries.

userStories.title: Build live single-stage review
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the live review path to use one single-stage workspace so that runtime UX matches the agreed annotation screen instead of the old split-pane harness.
acceptanceCriteria: Live review path no longer renders separate playback and exact-frame panes as the primary UX
Single-stage review surface keeps canonical frame loading and paused-only mutating actions intact
Existing live manual-box and SAM2 actions remain reachable from the new layout
Browser verification proves the live single-stage review surface works
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Build live single-stage review, Auditing m-2 and m-2a code gaps 2026-04-21, Review Workspace Ergonomics, Frontend Interaction Spec, Testing annotation foundation and manual box workflow, Testing SAM2 shell and runtime. Treat `live-review-app.tsx` as legacy harness to migrate, not final UI.

userStories.title: Add review navigation controls
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want useful landing, frame-jump, keyboard, and opacity controls on the live review path so that m-2 reviewer ergonomics become real.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening live review lands on a useful canonical frame without manual frame entry
Reviewer can jump between annotated frames or keyframes without repeated raw frame typing
Keyboard shortcuts and mask-opacity controls are available on the live review path
Browser verification proves the live control workflow works
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Add review navigation controls, Auditing m-2 and m-2a code gaps 2026-04-21, m-2: Review Workspace Completion, Review Workspace Ergonomics, Frontend Interaction Spec, frontend-integration-tests, e2e-tests. Reuse manifest `annotated_frames` and `keyframes` before adding new frame-summary routes.

userStories.title: Re-review m-2 and m-2a code and grow backlog
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the milestone-audit loop to run again after the new fix stories so that repo truth is checked against m-2 and m-2a after the runtime gaps move.
acceptanceCriteria: Review current code against `m-2: Review Workspace Completion` and `m-2a: Mockup UI Shell` after the new fix stories land
Write one fresh durable report note that says what is good, what is missing, what is wrong, and what should improve
Create one todo task note per concrete fix chosen from that report
Append matching fix stories to `tools/ralph/prd.json`
Append one cloned re-review story after the newly added fix stories
Record exactly which report note and which new tasks or stories were created
userStories.notes: Read memories first: Re-review m-2 and m-2a code and grow backlog, Auditing m-2 and m-2a code gaps 2026-04-21, Ship review summary contracts, Wire live library shell, Build live single-stage review, Add review navigation controls, Review Workspace Ergonomics, Frontend Interaction Spec.
